questions.question: What does MCP's OAuth profile require, beyond vanilla OAuth 2.1?
options: Client-credentials flow as the default
Authorization code + PKCE only, mandatory resource indicators on every token request, and published protected-resource metadata
API keys rotated every 24 hours
Biometric confirmation per tool call
questions.explanation: The 2025-11-25 spec profiles OAuth 2.1 strictly: no implicit flow, no default client-credentials, RFC 8707 resource indicators on every token request, and RFC 9728 metadata so clients can discover the authorization server from just the resource URL.

questions.question: What attack do resource indicators (RFC 8707) prevent?
options: Brute-forcing the user's password
The confused deputy: a malicious Server A replaying the client's token against Server B — the token's aud pins it to its intended audience
SQL injection in the token endpoint
Phishing of the consent screen
questions.explanation: The resource parameter at token request time bakes aud: "https://notes.example.com" into the token. Any other server receiving that token checks the audience and rejects it — tokens are no longer bearer-anywhere.

questions.question: In the PKCE flow, what does the code_verifier/code_challenge pair accomplish?
options: It encrypts the access token
It defeats authorization-code interception: the token request must present the verifier whose SHA256 matches the challenge sent at authorization time
It replaces the user's password
It compresses the redirect URL
questions.explanation: An attacker who intercepts the authorization code still can't redeem it, because the token endpoint validates the code_verifier against the code_challenge hash from step one — and only the legitimate client holds the verifier.

questions.question: How does step-up authorization (SEP-835) work when a notes:read client needs to delete a note?
options: The server silently grants the higher scope
The server returns 403 with WWW-Authenticate naming the missing scope; the client runs a consent prompt and mini OAuth flow for just that scope, then retries
The client must restart the entire session from initialize
The user must email the server administrator
questions.explanation: The insufficient_scope error names exactly what's missing (scope="notes:delete"). The client escalates with user consent for the increment — least-privilege by default, scope added only at the moment of need.

questions.question: What does the .well-known/oauth-protected-resource document (RFC 9728) give clients?
options: The server's source code
Discovery: which authorization servers issue tokens for this resource and which scopes exist — so the client only needs the resource URL to start
A list of all registered users
Rate-limit quotas
questions.explanation: The resource server publishes its authorization_servers and scopes_supported. Clients bootstrap the whole flow from one URL instead of out-of-band configuration.
